segments.title: DSI Drug and Alcohol Testing Contract One-Year Extension for CDL Compliance
segments.summary: One-year extension of contract with DSI for random drug/alcohol testing and CDL testing to maintain federal compliance. Originally a 2+1+1 year agreement. HR Director confirms satisfactory service. Will go to RFP when contract expires.
processing_notes: Rick Alceiver not in entity registry — HR Director

segments.title: Resolution Urging New York State Support for NY For All Act
segments.summary: Council member introduces resolution urging NY State Senate, Assembly, and Governor to support the New York For All Act. Seeks co-sponsors from fellow councilors; no immediate co-signers at this session.

political_signal.description: Council member advocates for state-level immigrant protection legislation

processing_notes: Presenter identified as Dr. Chol Majok from 'Council mach' transcript reference
NY For All Act = state-level legislation, no city department involvement

segments.title: OCRRA Battery Recycling Site Expansion and Park Memorial Donations
segments.summary: Parks Commissioner presents OCRRA battery recycling program expanding to four community center sites (Southwest, Northeast, Cecil, Magnarelli). Council acknowledges responsiveness to site concerns. Four park donations accepted: $2,000 from Eastwood Neighborhood Association for Cummings Field bench, $1,054 from Syracuse Inner Rotary Club for Kirk Park bench and memorial plaque honoring Al Turner, two $100 TNT donations for Franklin Square plaques honoring Ben Wash and TNT community contributions.

speakers.name: Sisha Bird
speakers.role: Commissioner of Parks, Recreation and Youth Programs

department_refs: Department of Parks, Recreation and Youth Programs
processing_notes: Dr. Chol Majok shares personal tribute to Al Turner — On Point for College volunteer who drove him to Buffalo for citizenship ceremony
TNT = Tomorrow's Neighborhood Today
OCRRA battery kiosk placement connects to Jan 12 study session discussion (xjf9vWa6BjQ)

segments.title: Fire Department Equipment, EMS Ambulance Billing, First Responder Wellness, and Training Center Usage
segments.summary: Multiple fire and police items: (1) SPD Deputy Chief presents CBH Group agreement for first responder resiliency speaker event hosted jointly with Onondaga County Sheriff's Department; (2) Fire Chief requests aerial ladder truck purchase under regular replacement program — 10-truck fleet with 10-year frontline/15-year total lifecycle, rotating by station usage; (3) Multimed third-party ambulance billing contract extension — Fire Dept has operated ambulance service since 1950s, expanded full-time during pandemic due to private ambulance staffing shortages, 2 ambulances with ~40 paramedics and 300+ EMTs; (4) NYSAFC annual conference hosting at training center for $8,500. Council requests apparatus replacement data and training center revenue history.

political_signal.description: Council requests detailed data on fire apparatus replacement history and training center revenue generation potential

entity_refs.name: New York State Association of Fire Chiefs

processing_notes: Finance and Law dept detections are false positives — mentioned in context of billing process and corp counsel insurance review, not as presenting departments
AMR = American Medical Response, private ambulance provider
Council interest in training center as revenue-generating asset
First responder wellness event connects to Jan 7 study session fire EAP discussion (-ZsKhcHXTd4)

segments.title: Appraisal Authorization for 725 Harrison Street (Former Washington Irving School)
segments.summary: Department of Assessment requests authorization to hire appraiser for 725 Harrison Street, the former Washington Irving School used as school district administrative offices. Building approaching its 100th birthday. Property was part of a 6-parcel RFP package issued approximately two years ago (3 for relocation, 3 for disposition); no transactions closed yet. Johnson Center relocated but redevelopment continues. Council members emphasize the building's historical significance as one of the last 15 historic school buildings in Syracuse.

political_signal.description: Council questions RFP disposition progress and emphasizes historic preservation requirements for centenarian school building

processing_notes: detected_departments flagged Permits and Development but presenting department is Assessment
Last appraisal was in 2000 — 26 years ago
Full disclosure from council member about historical preservation advocacy across administrations

segments.title: Northland Pro Fiber Connection Extension for Council Meeting Broadcasting
segments.summary: IT Director requests extension of existing fiber agreement with Northland Pro for the connection between City Hall and WCNY on West Fayette Street used for recording and broadcasting council meetings. Extension needed while full RFP process is prepared. Connection is fiber-only and does not include post-production support. Council raises questions about YouTube live streaming quality; directed to contact WCNY directly.
processing_notes: Council interest in YouTube live streaming quality — relevant to public transparency
WCNY handles post-production and TV broadcasting of council meetings

segments.title: Southwest Community Center Fiber Upgrade for E-Gaming and Property Settlement Ordinance Correction
segments.summary: Two items: (1) IT presents agreement with Spectrum to upgrade Southwest Community Center from coax to fiber connection to support Parks Department e-gaming program, following similar upgrade at Wilson/McBride Street; (2) Department of Assessment corrects settlement ordinance — need to rescind incorrect ordinance reference and amend original Ord. #942 from March 2024 with refund amount of $1,523.89. Assistant Corporation Counsel Trevor McDaniel confirms corrected letter with refund amount being submitted.
department_refs: Department of Parks, Recreation and Youth Programs
processing_notes: E-gaming initiative expanding to multiple community centers — Wilson/McBride already upgraded
Settlement ordinance correction is administrative cleanup, not new financial obligation

[00:34:59] explain it.
[00:35:00] >> Okay.
[00:35:01] Is it owned by the same person?
[00:35:04] >> Um, good question.
[00:35:07] >> Oh, land bank. It says land
[00:35:09] [clears throat] bank.
[00:35:09] >> Oh, it's a land bank.
[00:35:12] >> And what it would do, too, is hopefully
[00:35:14] allow future development. But there's
[00:35:16] also an existing fence that's kind of
[00:35:20] down that property line. It may cross
[00:35:21] it. This would eliminate that problem as
[00:35:24] well.
[00:35:25] >> Okay.
[00:35:28] >> Thank you. and 48 is a resolution to
[00:35:30] reappoint Linda Bats to the CRB.
[00:35:34] I just want to make sure you all be
[00:35:36] aware of that there are one appointment
[00:35:38] is still open and and CRB like like you
[00:35:42] guys to really get into it because at
[00:35:44] this point between you you the mayor or
[00:35:47] CRB can appoint someone because we're
[00:35:50] way over what the legislation says and
[00:35:53] um and I think there's another mayoral
[00:35:56] appointment
[00:35:57] >> that that's the mayor's appointment
[00:35:58] that's open isn't it? No third districts
[00:36:02] I think the mayor I think there's a
[00:36:03] mayor appointment um
[00:36:06] >> two I think yeah so two
[00:36:12] >> just to add before the meeting close uh
[00:36:13] Joe Joe Barry first assistant
[00:36:15] corporation council good good afternoon
[00:36:18] >> uh law and SPD are prepared to have an
[00:36:22] initial discussion about the legal
[00:36:24] aspects of the axon contract if you'd
[00:36:27] like to add that to executive session.
[00:36:28] Sure.
[00:36:30] >> At this time. Thank you.
[00:36:32] >> Thanks, Joe. I'm all set.
[00:36:43] >> Okay. And then we motion to move to

[00:36:50] >> Oh, legal advice.
[00:36:50] >> Oh, legal advice. Okay.
segments.title: Lot Alteration, CRB Reappointment, and Axon Contract Legal Preparation
segments.summary: Three items: (1) Deputy Commissioner of Code Enforcement presents lot alteration at 153-159 to straighten diagonal property line between occupied house and adjacent Greater Syracuse Land Bank lot for R3 zone compliance, eliminating split-zone and existing fence encroachment; (2) Resolution to reappoint Linda Bats to Citizen Review Board — council notes multiple CRB vacancies from both council and mayoral appointments are overdue per legislation; (3) First Assistant Corporation Counsel announces Law Department and SPD prepared to discuss Axon contract legal aspects in executive session.

political_signal.description: Council highlights unfilled CRB appointments from both council and mayor beyond legislative deadlines; Axon contract legal review signals careful oversight

speakers.name: Jake Dishaw
speakers.role: Deputy Commissioner of Code Enforcement and Zoning

processing_notes: Axon contract discussion moved to executive session — substantive legal aspects not publicly recorded
CRB legislation allows council, mayor, or CRB itself to appoint when vacancies are overdue
Lot alteration policy: avoid split-zone parcels on resub or lot alteration
Axon discussion connects to Feb 6 Joint Public Safety committee meeting (b75K4ejDfY4)

departments_found: Department of Parks, Recreation and Youth Programs
